title: GMAW welding parameters for carbon steel
content: For GMAW (MIG/MAG) on Q345R carbon steel: ER50-6 wire, 1.2mm diameter, Ar+CO2 (80/20) shielding gas, DCEP polarity. Current range 180-240A, voltage 22-28V, travel speed 25-35 cm/min, heat input 0.8-1.6 kJ/mm. Wire feed speed should be matched to current setting.
limitations: Reference values for typical Q345R GMAW applications. Confirm against governing standard and project-specific PQR.

title: GMAW shielding gas selection guide
content: GMAW shielding gas selection: For carbon steel, use Ar+CO2 (75-95% Ar, balance CO2). Pure CO2 is also acceptable but produces more spatter. For stainless steel, use Ar+CO2 (98/2) or Ar+O2 (99/1). Never use Ar+CO2 mixtures designed for SMAW with GMAW process.
limitations: General guidance only. Gas selection depends on material grade, thickness, and transfer mode.

title: SMAW electrode selection for carbon steel
content: SMAW (stick welding) electrode selection: J422 (E4303) for general-purpose carbon steel welding, AC/DC. J507 (E5015) for low-hydrogen requirements, DCEP only. J506 (E5016) for low-hydrogen, AC/DC. Electrode diameter: 2.5mm for thin plate, 3.2mm for 6-12mm, 4.0mm for 12mm+.
limitations: J422 is NOT compatible with GMAW process. J422 is a SMAW electrode only.

title: GTAW welding parameters
content: GTAW (TIG) welding: Use pure Ar or Ar+He shielding gas. Filler wire ER50-6 for carbon steel, ER308L for stainless. DCEN polarity (straight polarity). Current range 80-200A depending on thickness. Travel speed 10-25 cm/min. Lower heat input than GMAW/SMAW.
limitations: GTAW parameters vary significantly with joint type, position, and material thickness.

title: Preheat and PWHT requirements
content: Preheat requirements depend on material carbon equivalent, thickness, and hydrogen content of consumable. For Q345R (CE~0.40): no preheat required below 25mm thickness with low-hydrogen electrodes. Above 25mm, preheat to 100-150°C. Interpass temperature max 250-300°C for most carbon steels. PWHT: typically 600-650°C for 1 hour per 25mm thickness when required by code.
limitations: These are general guidelines. Actual thermal requirements must be determined by the applicable code (ASME IX, AWS D1.1, etc.) and qualified WPS.

title: Q345R material properties
content: Q345R is a Chinese pressure vessel steel grade (GB 713). Yield strength ≥345MPa, tensile strength 490-620MPa. Carbon equivalent CE(IIW) ≈ 0.38-0.42. Weldability: good, no special preheat required for thickness <25mm. Common applications: boilers, pressure vessels, storage tanks. Similar to ASTM A516 Gr.70.
limitations: Properties vary with thickness and heat treatment. Always verify with mill certificate.

title: Weld joint types and preparation
content: Common joint types: Butt joint (对接焊) - V-groove for plate >6mm, single bevel for 6-12mm, double bevel for >20mm. Fillet joint (角焊缝) - T-joint or lap joint. Corner joint (角接). Edge joint (卷边接头). Groove angle 60°±5° for V-groove. Root gap 0-3mm. Root face 1-2mm.
limitations: Joint design must comply with the applicable welding code and WPS.

title: Welding positions
content: Welding positions per AWS/ASME: Flat (平焊, 1G/1F) - easiest, highest deposition rate. Horizontal (横焊, 2G/2F) - moderate difficulty. Vertical (立焊, 3G/3F) - requires technique adjustment, lower current. Overhead (仰焊, 4G/4F) - most difficult, lowest current. Position affects current, voltage, and travel speed selection.
limitations: Position-specific parameters should be qualified per applicable code.

title: Heat input calculation
content: Heat input formula: HI (kJ/mm) = (Voltage × Current × 60) / (Travel Speed × 1000). For Q345R, typical heat input range: 0.8-2.5 kJ/mm. Excessive heat input can degrade mechanical properties. Insufficient heat input may cause lack of fusion. Heat input must be controlled within the qualified WPS range.
limitations: Heat input is a derived value. The actual range must be established by procedure qualification.
